description_long: This is a medium-sized, roughly cuboidal white rock with a chalky, matte surface and irregular, angular faces. The rock displays a blocky structure with visible layering and some subtle translucency, suggesting it may be composed of gypsum or a similar mineral. One side of the rock features a handwritten label with the code 'MIM 3226', indicating cataloging or collection for study or display. The surface is predominantly white with faint grayish undertones and minor imperfections, such as chips and small cracks. The rock's edges are mostly blunt, with some sharper corners and a few smoother, worn areas. Its overall appearance is utilitarian, and it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A blocky, white rock with a matte, chalky surface and a handwritten label, featuring angular faces and subtle layering.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's most prominent face with the handwritten label 'MIM 3226' clearly visible, highlighting its blocky structure and layered texture.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the rock's angular, stepped surfaces and reveals more of its layered, crystalline structure without the label visible.
description_view_2: This side displays a smoother, less angular face with subtle translucency and minor surface imperfections.
description_view_3: This view shows a broad, relatively flat face with the label partially visible on the edge, and highlights the rock's overall thickness.
